sourceMovie.title: Natural Born Killers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Natural Born Killers works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Natural Born Killers clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Natural Born Killers?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Nightcrawler, The House That Jack Built—each shares DNA with Natural Born Killers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Natural Born Killers?
faq.answer: Natural Born Killers (1994) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Natural Born Killers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
